revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=小池大塚古墳 |画像= |所在地=千葉県山武郡芝山町小池 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|scale:20000}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=墳丘全長72m |築造年代=6世紀末 |出土品=直刀、鉄鏃、鉄釘、ガラス小玉、須恵器、土師器 |史跡指定= |埋葬施設= }} '''小池大塚古墳'''（こいけおおつかこふん）は、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[芝山町]]小池にある[[古墳]]である。
== 概要 == 本古墳は、[[九十九里平野]]中央を流れる[[木戸川 (千葉県)|木戸川]]上流部東岸の[[台地]]上に存在する[[前方後円墳]]で、墳丘全長72メートル、後円部径46.5メートル、前方部長35メートル、前方部幅51メートル、くびれ部幅43メートル、後円部高5.2メートル、前方部高5.7メートルを測り、盾形周溝を含めた全長は約90メートルある。[[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）に[[早稲田大学]]考古学研究室によって発掘調査され、[[1988年]]（昭和63年）には[[千葉県教育委員会]]による墳丘測量が行われている。
埋葬施設は、くびれ部南側に開口する砂岩切石積み両袖型の[[横穴式石室]]で、全長は5.25メートル、石室内は[[盗掘]]を受けていた模様であるが、直刀、鉄鏃、鉄釘、ガラス小玉、須恵器、土師器などの遺物が検出されている。[[埴輪]]は認められておらず[[6世紀]]末葉の最終末の前方後円墳とみられている。周辺には[[円墳]]3基の所在が確認され、小池古墳群あるいは船塚古墳群とも称され、本古墳は船塚2号墳とも呼ばれる。また、町境を越えた南側の[[横芝光町]]中台には[[芝山古墳群]]が隣接しており、芝山古墳群の殿塚・姫塚古墳に後続して造営されたものと推察されている。
== 参考文献 == * 『続日本古墳大辞典』 [[東京堂出版]]、2002年。ISBN 4-490-10599-1
== 関連項目 == * [[古墳時代]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html 武社国（むさのくに）の古墳]
{{DEFAULTSORT:こいけおおつかこふん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:芝山町]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] {{japanese-history-stub}}

{{日本の古墳 |名称=小池大塚古墳 |画像= |所在地=千葉県山武郡芝山町小池 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=墳丘全長72m |築造年代=6世紀末 |出土品=直刀、鉄鏃、鉄釘、ガラス小玉、須恵器、土師器 |史跡指定= |埋葬施設= }} '''小池大塚古墳'''（こいけおおつかこふん）は、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[芝山町]]小池にある[[古墳]]である。
== 概要 == 本古墳は、[[九十九里平野]]中央を流れる[[木戸川 (千葉県)|木戸川]]上流部東岸の[[台地]]上に存在する[[前方後円墳]]で、墳丘全長72メートル、後円部径46.5メートル、前方部長35メートル、前方部幅51メートル、くびれ部幅43メートル、後円部高5.2メートル、前方部高5.7メートルを測り、盾形周溝を含めた全長は約90メートルある。[[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）に[[早稲田大学]]考古学研究室によって発掘調査され、[[1988年]]（昭和63年）には[[千葉県教育委員会]]による墳丘測量が行われている。
埋葬施設は、くびれ部南側に開口する砂岩切石積み両袖型の[[横穴式石室]]で、全長は5.25メートル、石室内は[[盗掘]]を受けていた模様であるが、直刀、鉄鏃、鉄釘、ガラス小玉、須恵器、土師器などの遺物が検出されている。[[埴輪]]は認められておらず[[6世紀]]末葉の最終末の前方後円墳とみられている。周辺には[[円墳]]3基の所在が確認され、小池古墳群あるいは船塚古墳群とも称され、本古墳は船塚2号墳とも呼ばれる。また、町境を越えた南側の[[横芝光町]]中台には[[芝山古墳群]]が隣接しており、芝山古墳群の殿塚・姫塚古墳に後続して造営されたものと推察されている。
== 参考文献 == * 『続日本古墳大辞典』 [[東京堂出版]]、2002年。ISBN 4-490-10599-1
== 関連項目 == * [[古墳時代]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]]
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html 武社国（むさのくに）の古墳]

{{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html Tombs of Musa Province] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shibayama Town]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern ... 5 KB (641 words) - 21:13, 15 January[[Category:original pages]][[Category:most recent 100 pages]][[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Original pages]][[Category:Most recent 100 pages]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:小池大塚古墳]][[ja:小池大塚古墳]] 2025

Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html Tombs of Musa Province] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shibayama Town]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern ... 5 KB (641 words) - 21:13, 15 January [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Koike Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]][[Category:Koike Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution 2]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:小池大塚古墳]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] 2025

Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html Tombs of Musa Province] [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shibayama Town]] [[Category:Keyhole-shaped tumuli]] {{japanese-history-stub}} [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} upstream on the eastern ... 5 KB (641 words) - 21:13, 15 January [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Koike Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Koike Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:小池大塚古墳]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Koike Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] 2025

Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Burial Mounds, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of burial mounds in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient burial mound ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped burial mound ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | Image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, Haji ware | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue ware, and Haji ware have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, Haji pottery | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}}) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, and Haji pottery have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, Haji pottery | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=draft}}) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=draft}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, and Haji pottery have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪|12=draft}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|12=draft}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, and Haji pottery have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|ja|埴輪}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html Tombs of Musa Province] {{japanese-history-stub}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|ja|芝山古墳群|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern ... 5 KB (641 words) - 21:13, 15 January {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shibayama Town]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Koike Otsuka Kofun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:小池大塚古墳]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Koike Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] [[sn:Koike Otsuka Kofun]]

revision.comment: 6 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:小池大塚古墳 up to rev 83477344
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=小池大塚古墳 |画像={{画像募集中|cat=山武郡}} |所在地=千葉県山武郡芝山町小池 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} |形状=前方後円墳 |規模=墳丘全長72m |築造年代=6世紀末 |出土品=直刀、鉄鏃、鉄釘、ガラス小玉、須恵器、土師器 |史跡指定= |埋葬施設= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=小池大塚
古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''小池大塚古墳'''（こいけおおつかこふん）は、[[千葉県]][[山武郡]][[芝山町]]小池にある[[古墳]]である。 == 概要 == 本古墳は、[[九十九里平野]]中央を流れる[[木戸川 (千葉県)|木戸川]]上流部東岸の[[台地]]上に存在する[[前方後円墳]]で、墳丘全長72メートル、後円部径46.5メートル、前方部長35メートル、前方部幅51メートル、くびれ部幅43メートル、後円部高5.2メートル、前方部高5.7メートルを測り、盾形周溝を含めた全長は約90メートルある。[[1966年]]（[[昭和]]41年）に[[早稲田大学]]考古学研究室によって発掘調査され、[[1988年]]（昭和63年）には[[千葉県教育委員会]]による墳丘測量が行われている。 埋葬施設は、くびれ部南側に開口する砂岩切石積み両袖型の[[横穴式石室]]で、全長は5.25メートル、石室内は[[盗掘]]を受けていた模様であるが、直刀、鉄鏃、鉄釘、ガラス小玉、須恵器、土師器などの遺物が検出されている。[[埴輪]]は認められておらず[[6世紀]]末葉の最終末の前方後円墳とみられている。周辺には[[円墳]]3基の所在が確認され、小池古墳群あるいは船塚古墳群とも称され、本古墳は船塚2号墳とも呼ばれる。また、町境を越えた南側の[[横芝光町]]中台には[[芝山古墳群]]が隣接しており、芝山古墳群の殿塚・姫塚古墳に後続して造営されたものと推察されている。 == 参考文献 == * 『続日本古墳大辞典』 [[東京堂出版]]、2002年。ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == 関連項目 == * [[古墳時代]] * [[日本の古墳一覧]] == 外部リンク == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html 武社国（むさのくに）の古墳] {{DEFAULTSORT:こいけおおつかこふん}} [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:芝山町の歴史]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] {{japanese-history-stub}}
