Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba)|qq=}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, and Haji pottery have been recovered. The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|qq=}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html Tombs of Musa Province] {{japanese-history-stub}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] upstream on the eastern ... 5 KB (641 words) - 21:13, 15 January {{translated page|ja|小池大塚古墳|version=83477344|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient tumuli in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shibayama Town]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:kofun stubs]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Koike Otsuka Kofun]] [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] [[Category:千葉県の古墳]] [[Category:山武郡の画像提供依頼]]

Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba)|qq=}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, and Haji pottery have been recovered. The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba)|qq=}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, and Haji pottery have been recovered. The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba)|qq=}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, and Haji pottery have been recovered. The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.683333|N|140.416667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11462258]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11462258]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, Haji pottery | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba)|qq=}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, and Haji pottery have been recovered. The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html Tombs of Musa Province] {{japanese-history-stub}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] upstream on the eastern ... 5 KB (641 words) - 21:13, 15 January {{translated page|ja|小池大塚古墳|version=83477344|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Image Requests for Sambu District]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] {{wikidata link|Q11462258}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba)|qq=}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, and Haji pottery have been recovered. The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html Tombs of Musa Province] {{japanese-history-stub}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] upstream on the eastern ... 5 KB (641 words) - 21:13, 15 January {{translated page|ja|小池大塚古墳|version=83477344|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Image Requests for Sambu District]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] {{wikidata link|Q11462258}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}) is an ancient Kofun ([[:en:Kofun|Kofun]]) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ([[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]]) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba)|qq=}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, and Haji pottery have been recovered. The absence of [[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]] suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html Tombs of Musa Province] {{japanese-history-stub}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the [[:en:Shibayama Kofun Cluster|Shibayama Kofun Cluster]] upstream on the eastern ... 5 KB (641 words) - 21:13, 15 January {{translated page|ja|小池大塚古墳|version=83477344|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11462258}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Image Requests for Sambu District]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[ja:小池大塚古墳]] [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.683333|N|140.416667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11462258]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11462258]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, Haji pottery | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba)|qq=}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, and Haji pottery have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html Tombs of Musa Province] {{japanese-history-stub}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern ... 5 KB (641 words) - 21:13, 15 January {{translated page|ja|小池大塚古墳|version=83477344|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11462258}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Image Requests for Sambu District]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]

[[ja:小池大塚古墳]] [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|35.683333|N|140.416667|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}}} {{Infobox ancient tumulus | Name = Koike Otsuka Kofun | image = {{image request|cat=Sanmu District}} | Location = Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture | Coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:20000|display=inline,title}} | Shape = Keyhole-shaped tumulus | Size = Mound length: 72 meters | Construction Period = Late 6th century | Excavated Artifacts = Straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, Haji pottery | Burial Facility = | Map = {{Location map|Japan Chiba|label=Koike Otsuka
Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba)|qq=}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, and Haji pottery have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html Tombs of Musa Province] {{japanese-history-stub}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|WD=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern ... 5 KB (641 words) - 21:13, 15 January {{translated page|ja|小池大塚古墳|version=83477344|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11462258}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Image Requests for Sambu District]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:pages]]

Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=00|lon_deg=140|lon_min=25|lon_sec=00|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{Coord|35|41|00|N|140|25|00|E}}}} }} '''Koike Otsuka Kofun''' ({{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Otsuka Kofun|qq=}}) is an ancient Kofun ({{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}}) located in Koike, Shibayama Town, Sanbu District, Chiba Prefecture. == Overview == This tumulus is a keyhole-shaped Kofun ({{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}) situated on a plateau along the eastern bank of the upper reaches of the {{ill|Kido River (Chiba)|ja|木戸川 (千葉県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kido River (Chiba)|qq=}} in the Kujukuri Plain. The mound measures 72 meters in total length, with a posterior circular diameter of 46.5 meters, a frontal length of 35 meters, a frontal width of 51 meters, a constricted width of 43 meters, a posterior height of 5.2 meters, and a frontal height of 5.7 meters. Including the surrounding shield-shaped moat, the overall length extends to approximately 90 meters. The site was excavated by Waseda University's Archaeological Research Laboratory in 1966 and surveyed by the Chiba Prefectural Board of Education in 1988. The burial facility is a double-sleeved horizontal stone chamber ({{ill|Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-shiki sekishitsu|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}) constructed of cut sandstone blocks, with an overall length of 5.25 meters. Although evidence of looting is apparent, artifacts such as straight swords, iron arrowheads, iron nails, glass beads, Sue pottery, and Haji pottery have been recovered. The absence of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} suggests the tumulus was constructed in the final phase of keyhole-shaped mounds during the late 6th century. Three additional round tumuli ({{ill|Enfun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Enfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}) have been identified nearby, collectively referred to as the Koike Kofun Cluster or Funazuka Kofun Cluster. This tumulus is also known as Funazuka No. 2. Additionally, the neighboring {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} in Yokoshibahikari Town to the south suggests this site was built subsequent to Shibayama’s Tonozuka and Himezuka Tumuli. == References == * ''Zoku Nihon Kofun Daijiten'' (Encyclopedia of Japanese Kofuns, Continued). Tokyo-do Publishing, 2002. ISBN 4-490-10599-1 == Related Articles == * {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} * {{ill|List of Kofuns in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofuns in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11505809}} == External Links == * [http://www.haniwakan.com/kenkyu/boso/musa.html Tombs of Musa Province] {{japanese-history-stub}} * [[Yamamuro Himezuka Kofun]] In contrast to nearby kofun clusters, such as the {{ill|Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Otsuka Kofun|lt=Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|小池大塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koike Ōtsuka Kofun|qq=}} and the {{ill|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|en|Shibayama Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11614635|lt=Shibayama Kofun Cluster|1=Shibayama Kofun Cluster}} upstream on the eastern ... 5 KB (641 words) - 21:13, 15 January {{translated page|ja|小池大塚古墳|version=83477344|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:小池大塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11462258}} [[Category:Image Requests for Sambu District]] [[Category:Kofun of Chiba prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shibayama]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Zenpokoenfun]]
